title: A Cost-Optimized 5-Protein Panel for Systemic Lupus Erythematosus Diagnosis
author: Wenhua Lv
materials: Predict probabilities on the test set. (
Compare ProtRS with PRS and clinical risk factors
Extract the standard PRS for SLE.

steps.title: Extract control individuals (n=48,036) from non-cancer...
steps.instruction: Extract control individuals (n=48,036) from non-cancer self-reported population without SLE or related autoimmune conditions.

steps.title: For each of the 2,923 plasma...
steps.instruction: For each of the 2,923 plasma proteins, perform mean imputation for missing values (replace NA with the protein’s mean across all samples).

steps.title: Center and scale each protein to...
steps.instruction: Center and scale each protein to zero mean and unit variance.

steps.title: Obtain UK Biobank approved data
steps.instruction: Obtain UK Biobank approved data. Extract SLE cases using ICD-9 / ICD-10 and self-report. Exclude other autoimmune diseases.

steps.title: Identification of SLE‑associated proteins using LASSO with BCCS (1,000 iterations)
steps.instruction: Identification of SLE‑associated proteins using LASSO with BCCS (1,000 iterations)

steps.title: Set random seed for reproducibility (e
steps.instruction: Set random seed for reproducibility (e.g., `set.seed(123)`).

steps.title: Randomly sample from the control pool...
steps.instruction: Randomly sample from the control pool a number of controls equal to the number of SLE cases (n=544) → balanced case‑control set (1:1). (

steps.title: Run LASSO logistic regression (`glmnet`, family="binomial", alpha=
steps.instruction: Run LASSO logistic regression (`glmnet`, family="binomial", alpha=

steps.title: with disease status as outcome and...
steps.instruction: with disease status as outcome and all 2,923 proteins as predictors. (

steps.title: Record the non‑zero coefficients and corresponding protein names
steps.instruction: Record the non‑zero coefficients and corresponding protein names.

steps.title: After 1,000 iterations, count the **...
steps.instruction: After 1,000 iterations, count the ** selection frequency ** for each protein (how many iterations it had non‑zero coefficient).

steps.title: Retain proteins selected in ** ≥500...
steps.instruction: Retain proteins selected in ** ≥500 iterations (≥50% frequency) ** as stable SLE‑associated biomarkers.

steps.title: For each retained protein, compute the...
steps.instruction: For each retained protein, compute the **final coefficient** as the median of its non‑zero coefficients across all iterations where it was selected.

steps.title: Calculate Protein Risk Score (ProtRS) and evaluate full 35‑protein model
steps.instruction: Calculate Protein Risk Score (ProtRS) and evaluate full 35‑protein model

steps.title: For each individual j, compute ProtRS...
steps.instruction: For each individual j, compute ProtRS = Σ(β_i × Prot_ij) where β_i is the final coefficient from Step 2.5, and Prot_ij is the centered+scaled protein level.

steps.title: Split the balanced set into training (70%) and testing (30%)
steps.instruction: Split the balanced set into training (70%) and testing (30%). (

steps.title: Fit a logistic regression model
steps.instruction: Fit a logistic regression model: `SLE ~ ProtRS + age + gender` on the training set. (

steps.title: Calculate AUC, accuracy, precision, sensitivity, specificity,...
steps.instruction: Calculate AUC, accuracy, precision, sensitivity, specificity, F1 using `pROC` and `caret`.

steps.title: Aggregate the 1,000 metrics
steps.instruction: Aggregate the 1,000 metrics: report median and 95% uncertainty interval (2.5th and 97.5th percentiles)

steps.title: Model PRS
steps.instruction: Model PRS: `SLE ~ PRS + age + gender`. (

steps.title: Model clinical
steps.instruction: Model clinical: `SLE ~ smoking + sleep_duration + insomnia + PM2.5 + age + gender`. (

steps.title: Categorise continuous variables as described in Methods
steps.instruction: Categorise continuous variables as described in Methods: PM2.5, PRS, ProtRS by quartiles; sleep duration into <7h, 7‑8h, >9h.

steps.title: Evaluate performance metrics for each model similarly
steps.instruction: Evaluate performance metrics for each model similarly. Compare bar plots.

steps.title: Rank the 35 stable proteins by...
steps.instruction: Rank the 35 stable proteins by **descending selection frequency** (from Step 2.3).

steps.title: Take the top k proteins
steps.instruction: Take the top k proteins. (

steps.title: Compute ProtRS_k as weighted sum using...
steps.instruction: Compute ProtRS_k as weighted sum using their median coefficients (from Step 2.5). (

steps.title: Repeat the 1,000 BCCS evaluation (Step 3
steps.instruction: Repeat the 1,000 BCCS evaluation (Step 3.

steps.title: Plot performance metrics (AUC, accuracy, etc
steps.instruction: Plot performance metrics (AUC, accuracy, etc.) against number of proteins.

steps.title: Identify the “ cost-effective optimum ”...
steps.instruction: Identify the “ cost-effective optimum ” where adding more proteins yields minimal gain. The top 5 proteins (TRIM21, SOD2, KLK3, IL15, ADIPOQ) achieve AUC=0.82 while reducing assay costs by ~87% compared to 35 proteins.

steps.title: For each risk factor (ProtRS, PRS, insomnia, sleep duration, PM2
steps.instruction: For each risk factor (ProtRS, PRS, insomnia, sleep duration, PM2.5, smoking), discretise variables as described (e.g., ProtRS by quartiles, lowest quartile as reference).

steps.title: Using the `graphPAF` R package function...
steps.instruction: Using the `graphPAF` R package function `PAF_calc_discrete()`, compute PAF with the formula: PAF = 1 – Σ (p_i / RR_i) where p_i = proportion in exposure level i, RR_i = relative risk compared to optimal level (estimated from logistic regression).

steps.title: Repeat for each of the 1,000...
steps.instruction: Repeat for each of the 1,000 BCCS iterations to obtain median PAF and 95% UI.

steps.title: with multiple imputation by chained equations...
steps.instruction: with multiple imputation by chained equations (`mice`), median imputation, or k-nearest neighbours (k=5, `VIM::kNN`). (

steps.title: Higher iteration stability (1) Repeat Step...
steps.instruction: Higher iteration stability (1) Repeat Step 2 with **10,000 BCCS iterations** for LASSO, retaining proteins selected in ≥50% of iterations. (2) Compare overlap with the 1,000‑iteration set using a Venn diagram (Figure S5). Expect >97% concordance.

description: Select the penalty parameter λ by 10‑fold cross‑validation using `lambda.min` (the λ giving minimum mean cross‑validated error). (
